arts.ttl: CONFIRMED lineups: Real Madrid vs Manchester City, 2025 Champions League
arts.sum: Real Madrid have published their starting lineup for the upcoming match against Manchester City in the return leg of the Champions League ’s Round of 32. Real Madrid starting XI: Courtois, Valverde, Rudiger, Asencio, Mendy, Tchouameni, Ceballos, Bellingham, Rodrygo, Vinicius, Mbappe.

arts.ttl: Real Madrid vs Manchester City - Champions League: Live score and team news as Pep's side look to bounce back from late double blow and overturn first-leg deficit
arts.sum: Follow Mail Sport's live blog of Real Madrid vs Manchester City in the Champions League with the Citizens bidding to overturn a first-leg deficit as they chase victory at the Bernabeu.

arts.ttl: Real Madrid transfer news: Carlo Ancelotti 'could help' Los Blancos 'land £75m centre-back' this summer
arts.sum: Real Madrid are allegedly considering moving for Everton centre-back Jarrad Branthwaite during this summer's transfer window.

arts.sum: The board at La Liga giants Real Madrid utilised quotes on the part of club legend Cristiano Ronaldo to defend Jude Bellingham in the aftermath of his recent red card.…

arts.ttl: Manchester City youth product in attack on Jude Bellingham ahead of match
arts.sum: Manchester City youth product Pablo Maffeo, now at Mallorca, may have stoked the fires ahead of their clash with Real Madrid in the Champions League, by hitting out at Jude Bellingham. AS cover comments from the defender today in which he accuses the midfielder of being a fake and admits he prefers Vinicius Junior to The post Manchester City youth product in attack on Jude Bellingham ahead of match appeared first on Sport Witness.

arts.ttl: Claudio Echeverri comments speak volumes as ‘angry’ Man City star vows to ‘take revenge’
arts.sum: Manchester City's new signing Claudio Echeverri has had some strong words for Brazil after he and his Argentinian team-mates were taunted by the South American U20 Championship winners

arts.ttl: Zubimendi HIJACK? Arsenal update as Real Madrid, Liverpool stance revealed
arts.sum: There is no risk of Liverpool or Real Madrid hijacking Arsenal's deal to sign Martin Zubimendi as things stand, according to a report. Zubimendi has a closer attachment to his team than most modern footballers and he rejected Liverpool last summer despite the fact that even Real Sociedad had seemingly accepted that he would likely leave.
